---
title: "Tablas por especie Epis y Foros"
author: "Edith"
date: "2026-03-10"
output: html_document
---
Epifitas
```{r }
setwd("C:/Users/edith clemente arena/Desktop/EPIFITAS -GRADIENTE/treeline-epiphytes/treeline-epiphytes")
#FOROFITOS
data <- read.csv("estrucPOB.csv", header = TRUE, sep = ";", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
hospepi <- data %>% ###TABLA DE DATOS POBLACIONALES
  dplyr::select(plot, elevation, forof,morphospe, family, genera_tree, dap, height)
str(hospepi)

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

tabla_epifitas <- hospepi %>%
  count(family, morphospe, elevation) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = elevation,
    values_from = n,
    values_fill = 0
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Total = rowSums(across(where(is.numeric)))
  ) %>%
  arrange(family, morphospe)
tabla_epifitas

write.csv(tabla_epifitas,
          "Epiphyte_species_checklist.csv",
          row.names = FALSE)
```

Revisar la taxonomia
```{r}
library(rgbif)
library(purrr)
nrow(hospepi)                     # número de individuos
length(unique(hospepi$morphospe)) # número de especies
species_list <- unique(hospepi$morphospe)# extraer lista unica de especies
length(species_list)
#Convertir nombres a UTF-8 válido
species_list <- unique(hospepi$morphospe)
species_list <- iconv(species_list,
                      from = "",
                      to = "UTF-8",
                      sub = "")
#limpiar especies
species_clean <- species_list %>%
  trimws() %>%                       # quitar espacios extremos
  gsub("\\s+", " ", .) %>%           # quitar dobles espacios
  gsub(" \\(.*\\)", "", .)           # quitar autores entre paréntesis
length(species_clean)##verifica si son 160
# Consultar taxonomia en GBIF
tax_check <- purrr::map_df(species_clean, function(x){

  Sys.sleep(0.2)

  res <- tryCatch(
    rgbif::name_backbone(name = x),
    error = function(e) NULL
  )

  if(is.null(res) || nrow(res) == 0){

    sci <- NA
    fam <- NA
    stat <- "not_found"

  } else {

    sci  <- if("scientificName" %in% names(res)) res$scientificName[1] else NA
    fam  <- if("family" %in% names(res)) res$family[1] else NA
    stat <- if("status" %in% names(res)) res$status[1] else NA

  }

  data.frame(
    input_name = x,
    scientificName = sci,
    family = fam,
    status = stat,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )

})
head(tax_check)#revisar resultados
#ver resultados 
tax_check %>%
  dplyr::select(input_name, scientificName, family, status)
#ver especies no encontradas
tax_check %>%
  filter(is.na(family))
#comparar familias con tu base original
original_tax <- hospepi %>%
  distinct(morphospe, family)
compare_tax <- original_tax %>%
  left_join(tax_check,
            by = c("morphospe" = "input_name"))
write.csv(compare_tax, "Revision de checklist.csv", row.names = FALSE)
#Detectar familias diferentes
compare_tax %>%
  filter(family.x != family.y)
#crear tabla taxonomica
tax_final <- tax_check %>%
  dplyr::select(
    species = scientificName,
    family,
    status
  )
tax_final
write.csv(tax_final, "taxonomia_epifitas.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```
NOTA: me sirvio solo para ver si las familias estaban correctas pero no coincide cuando revice powo.science.KEW

Revisar con POWO
```{r}
install.packages("taxize")
library(taxize)
library(dplyr)
library(purrr)
species_list <- unique(hospepi$morphospe)
length(species_list)
#Convertir nombres a UTF-8 válido
species_list <- unique(hospepi$morphospe)
species_list <- iconv(species_list,
                      from = "",
                      to = "UTF-8",
                      sub = "")
#Limpiar nombres
species_clean <- species_list |>
  trimws() |>
  gsub("\\s+", " ", x = _) |>
  gsub(" \\(.*\\)", "", x = _)
#consultar POWO
tax_powo <- map_df(species_clean, function(x){

  res <- tryCatch(
    taxize::classification(x, db = "powo"),
    error = function(e) NULL
  )

  if(is.null(res)){
    return(data.frame(
      species_input = x,
      family = NA
    ))
  }

  fam <- res[[1]] %>%
    filter(rank == "family")

  if(nrow(fam) == 0){
    fam_name <- NA
  } else {
    fam_name <- fam$name
  }

  data.frame(
    species_input = x,
    family = fam_name
  )

})
#Crear tabla final
tax_final <- tax_powo %>%
  rename(species = species_input) %>%
  arrange(family, species)
print(tax_final)
View(tax_final)
```


HOSPEDEROS
tabla de familia y especies revisando colocando la familia con GBIF
```{r}
data <- read.csv("estrucPOB.csv", header = TRUE, sep = ";", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
host <- data %>% ###TABLA DE DATOS POBLACIONALES
  dplyr::select(plot, elevation, forof,tree_name, genera_tree, dap, height)
str(host)
library(dplyr)
host_species <- host %>%
  distinct(tree_name)
#buscar la familia en gbif
library(rgbif)
library(purrr)

host_species$family <- map_chr(host_species$tree_name, function(x){

  res <- tryCatch(
    name_backbone(name = x),
    error = function(e) NULL
  )

  if(is.null(res) || !"family" %in% names(res) || length(res$family) == 0){
    NA_character_
  } else {
    res$family[1]
  }

})
#crea la tabla 
tax_host_final <- host_species %>%
  dplyr::rename(species = tree_name) %>%
  dplyr::select(family, species) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(family, species)
print(tax_host_final)#ver tabla
View(tax_final)
head(tax_final)

table(is.na(tax_final$family))
#guardar tabla
write.csv(tax_host_final, "host_species_table.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```

Tabla de hospederos por elevaciones
```{r}
library(dplyr)

host_unique <- host %>%
  distinct(forof, tree_name, elevation)
host_count <- host_unique %>%
  count(tree_name, elevation)
library(tidyr)

host_wide <- host_count %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = elevation,
    values_from = n,
    values_fill = 0
  )
host_table <- host_wide %>%
  left_join(tax_host_final, by = c("tree_name" = "species"))
host_table <- host_table %>%
  dplyr::select(family, tree_name, everything()) %>%
  arrange(family, tree_name)
write.csv(host_table, "host_table_elev.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```
